images.caption:
Figure 1.Decode-phase per-expert activation relative to the cross-domain mean,
for three MoE models along task (top) and language (bottom,
WildChat(Zhaoet al.,2024)) domains at each model’s most discriminative layer.
Darker is above-average (below-average clipped to white); experts are reordered
per panel into contiguous per-domain blocks. Each domain over-activates a
distinct subset of experts. Task domains:
Code(Chenet al.,2021; Zhuoet al.,2025; Laiet al.,2023; Austinet al.,2021),
Math(Cobbeet al.,2021; Hendryckset al.,2021b; Lightmanet al.,2023; Heet al.,2024; Linget al.,2017),
Medical(Jinet al.,2021,2019; Hendryckset al.,2021a), and
Legal(Chalkidiset al.,2022).

Figure 2.MoE layer latency scales with active experts, not batch
size (single MoE layer, one MI300X).

Figure 3.Prefill expert activation predicts decode activation. Each
point is one expert (normalized prefillxxvs. decodeyy, pooled over
domains); points near the diagonal are experts used about equally in
both phases.

Figure 4.Same-domain batches (blue) activate fewer experts per decode
step than mixed-domain (orange),
for task (top) and language (bottom) across three MoE models.

Figure 5.WildChat(Zhaoet al.,2024)request volume is heavily skewed: English and
Chinese alone are∼\sim75% of requests.

Figure 6.ELDRarchitecture: offline fitting of one centroid
per decode worker over expert signatures, then online routing
at the prefill?밺ecode handoff by signature similarity,
subject to load.

Figure 7.Signature qualityρ\rho(Eq.1) for six candidate
transformationsTT. Bars are the mean across six cells
(3 models×\times2 workloads); whiskers span the per-cell min/max.

Figure 8.Cumulativeρ\rho(Eq.1) versus the number of
layers kept under greedy layer selection. One panel per model;
task (blue) and language (orange) shown separately. The star
marks the peakN∗N^{*}chosen byELDR’s offline fit.

Figure 9.PCA of calibration signatures with Hungarian-balanced centroids
(K=8K{=}8for legibility), colored by task domain (top) or top-4
WildChat(Zhaoet al.,2024)language (bottom).

Figure 10.ELDRstores expert signatures at KV cache block granularity: the
signature cache is co-indexed with KV cache.
Figure 11.TPOT (median, p99) and median TTFT vs request rate on thetaskworkload at 8P16D.

Figure 12.TPOT (median, p99) and median TTFT vs request rate on thelanguageworkload at 8P16D.

Figure 13.Mean active experts per decode step on Qwen3-30B-A3B in the task domain on
an 8P16D cluster.

Figure 14.TPOT P50/P99 %Δ\Deltavs. RR atr=60r{=}60req/s (8P16D, six
(model, dataset) cells) for two signature transforms: the IDF-reweighted
top-kkcount (count⋅\cdotidf) vs. the continuous softmax
gate (gate-prob). Rest of the recipe
fixed (greedy mask, balancedKK-meansK=16K{=}16,τ=0.1\tau{=}0.1).
